prompt: I want a horizontal-scrolling case-studies section on my website. As the user scrolls vertically through this section, the inner content should translate horizontally. There should be 5 case studies, each is a full-viewport-width card. The horizontal scroll should feel a bit slower than 1:1 (so the user spends some time in the section). Write me the full React + TypeScript component. Use Motion. I already have the case studies array defined elsewhere.
assertions: Uses useScroll with target: ref and offset: ['start start', 'end end'] (or equivalent)
Uses useTransform to map scrollYProgress to a horizontal translation
Outer container has a tall height (e.g. 300vh or larger) to slow the scroll
Inner container uses position: sticky with top: 0 and overflow: hidden
Flex row of cards inside the sticky container with appropriate flex-shrink: 0 or fixed widths
Does not use any Motion+ components
